identity.name: Hong Kong
identity.nativeName: 香港特別行政區 (Heung Kong Dak Bit Hang Jing Keui) / Hong Kong SAR

definition.text: Hong Kong is a Special Administrative Region of the People’s Republic of China, located on the southern coast of China. It has a highly developed, service-oriented economy, historically rooted in trade, finance, and logistics. Hong Kong is a major global financial center and port, with strong legal and regulatory institutions inherited from its colonial history under the United Kingdom. In recent years, there has been a push to develop technology and innovation sectors alongside traditional finance and professional services.

statistics.value: Special Administrative Region (Under ‘One Country, Two Systems’)
statistics.label: System of Government

works.title: Actor Set Evolution of Hong Kong
works.description: Temporal mapping of Hong Kong’s actor space from colonial administration and trade networks to financial institutions, multinational corporates, and contemporary regulatory authorities.

works.title: Industrial and Trade Network Structure
works.description: Network analysis of Hong Kong’s financial, logistics, and emerging technology sectors, including integration into regional and global production and trade networks.

works.title: Regional Spatial-Administrative Structure
works.description: Geospatial representation of Hong Kong’s administrative districts, urban centers, ports, technology parks, and transport infrastructure.
